items.title: [types] Add Chunky DKG types and on-chain config
items.body:
## Summary
Adds the core Chunky DKG type definitions and on-chain configuration.

### New Types in `types/src/dkg/chunky_dkg.rs`:
- `ChunkyDKGConfig` - configuration with threshold config, public params, session metadata, encryption keys
- `ChunkyDKGSessionMetadata` - session info for Chunky DKG
- `ChunkyDKGStartEvent` - Move event for Chunky DKG start
- `ChunkyDKGTranscript` - transcript with metadata
- `CertifiedAggregatedChunkySubtranscript` - validated aggregated transcript with signature
- `ChunkyDKGSessionState` / `ChunkyDKGState` - on-chain state reflections
- `ChunkyDKG` - wrapper struct with deal/verify/aggregate helpers

### New Types in `types/src/on_chain_config/chunky_dkg_config.rs`:
- `ChunkyDKGConfigMoveStruct` - Move struct reflection
- `OnChainChunkyDKGConfig` - Off/V1 config variants with thresholds

### Other Changes:
- Added `ChunkyDKGStartEvent` conversion in `contract_event.rs`
- Exported `InputSecret`, `EncryptPubKey`, `PublicParameters` from aptos-dkg chunky module

## Test plan
- `cargo check -p aptos-types -p aptos-dkg` passes
- Type definitions only, no functional changes

## Type of Change
- [x] New feature

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Other (types crate, DKG infrastructure)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

items.title: [crypto] Rename SecretSharingConfig trait to TSecretSharingConfig
items.body:
## Summary

The name SecretSharingConfig makes it confusing for a trait name, because I usually associate config with a welldefined struct. Renaming to  TSecretSharingConfig to make this distinction clear.

- Renamed `SecretSharingConfig` trait to `TSecretSharingConfig` in aptos-crypto
- Updated all references in aptos-crypto, aptos-dkg, and aptos-batch-encryption crates

This renames the SecretSharingConfig trait to TSecretSharingConfig to follow the naming convention for traits (T prefix) and avoid confusion with associated types of the same name in PVSS transcript traits.

## Test plan
- `cargo check -p aptos-crypto -p aptos-dkg -p aptos-batch-encryption` passes
- No functional changes, pure rename refactoring

## Type of Change
- [x] Refactoring

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Other (crypto crates)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

items.title: [TSS] add support for randomness in local mode
items.body:
This fixes Transaction Simulation Session to support transactions that use on-chain randomness in local mode.

Previously, simulating random transactions would fail because the randomness seed was not set. Now we patch a random seed when initializing local sessions.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: change is scoped to local Transaction Simulation Session initialization, but it introduces nondeterministic seeding which could affect reproducibility of simulations/tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Local Transaction Simulation Sessions now **seed on-chain randomness** by writing a synthetic `PerBlockRandomness` config during `Session::init`, enabling simulation of transactions that call randomness APIs (remote/forked sessions intentionally remain unchanged).
> 
> Adds `rand` as a dependency, includes a regression test asserting the seed is present and 32 bytes, and bumps the Aptos CLI version/changelog to `7.14.2`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 9b41605e97d2291cf3574507e6bd0867b411c7a0. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [Encrypted Txn] Add TypeScript tests for encrypted transactions
items.body:
## Description
This PR adds typescript tests for the `aptos-batch-encryption` crate. The PR offers the following commits:
1. Add the new typescript tests (as taken from this PR: https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18238), and fix various lint issues.
2. Add a new github action (`rust-batch-encryption-tests`) which runs all tests in the `aptos-batch-encryption crate`. We've excluded these tests from other rust-based testing jobs, e.g., `rust-targeted-unit-tests`, to avoid issues with these tests requiring additional dependencies, e.g., `node.js`.

Once this PR lands, we need to do the following:
1. Update `aptos-core` to make the `rust-batch-encryption-tests` job land-blocking. I will do this.
2. Investigate why some of these tests are flaky (e.g., see this [run](https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/actions/runs/21445011564/job/61758383174?pr=18521)). Right now, this is fine because we allow retries.
3. Address any relevant comments identified by the github-code-quality bot below.

## Testing Plan
New and existing test infrastructure.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it adds a new PR-required CI job with Node/pnpm dependencies and changes `hash_g2_element` behavior/logging (switching to `clear_cofactor`), which could affect cryptographic correctness/performance if used outside tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `ts-batch-encrypt` TypeScript workspace (pnpm-based) plus Rust↔TS integration tests under `crates/aptos-batch-encryption/src/tests/typescript` to validate BCS/curve serialization and encryption/decryption compatibility by invoking TS via `pnpm exec tsx`.
> 
> Adjusts Rust test helpers (`blank_for_testing` / `new_for_testing`) to construct deterministic ciphertext/key fixtures for the cross-language tests, and updates `hash_g2_element` to use `clear_cofactor` (with added debug prints).
> 
> Updates CI to **exclude** `aptos-batch-encryption` from existing `rust-unit-tests`/t

items.title: [encrypted mempool] Add `verify_decryption_key` method to `BatchThresholdEncryption` trait
items.body:
This PR adds `verify_decryption_key` to the `BatchThresholdEncryption` trait. When a fullnode is replaying a block, it can use this method, along with the epoch's encryption key and the digest of all ciphertexts in the block, to verify that the decryption key was computed correctly.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Introduces decryption key verification to the batch threshold encryption interface and consolidates public key handling.
> 
> - Adds `verify_decryption_key` to `BatchThresholdEncryption` and implements it in `fptx.rs`, `fptx_succinct.rs`, and `fptx_weighted.rs`
> - Replaces `BIBEMasterPublicKey` wrapper with raw `G2Affine` across key derivation and consumers; updates constructors and tests (use `mpk` instead of `mpk.0`)
> - Centralizes shifted BLS verification as `key_derivation::verify_shifted_bls`; `EncryptionKey` and `AugmentedEncryptionKey` now use it in `verify_decryption_key`
> - Minor doc/comment additions and test updates to reflect the new API
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 99574e4e68dc0999c00b42e40ff771e9b8b78764. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [Orderbook] Return ClearinghouseStoppedMatching cancellation reason when matching stops
items.body:
When the clearinghouse signals to stop matching without providing a taker cancellation reason, return ClearinghouseStoppedMatching as the cancellation reason instead of none. This provides better visibility into why an order stopped matching.

Changes:
- Add ClearinghouseStoppedMatching enum variant to OrderCancellationReason
- Add is_clearinghouse_stopped_matching() helper function
- Return the new cancellation reason when should_stop is true and remaining_size > 0
- Add test for the new cancellation reason behavior

items.title: [vm] Fix async code state replay
items.body:
## Description

Fixes a race condition in async runtime type checks where trace replay could read modules from newer code state, causing sequential execution fallback.

The race condition can be identified in the following scenario (see added tests for POC). Transaction 1 executes with code state S1, then transaction 2 publishes new modules (S2), and finally transaction 1's post-commit replay runs using the current global module cache (S2) instead of the execution-time snapshot (S1). This caused trace replay invariant violation.
                                                                                                                  
This PR adds `SnapshotModuleView` that implements `ModuleStorage` by resolving modules exclusively from the transaction's `CapturedReads` read-set. Replay now uses `SnapshotModuleView` and not `LatestView` to resolve to older modules.

Fixes EPS-138

## How Has This Been Tested?

                                                                                                                  
Added a test that mimics race condition scenario. Without the fix, the test panics with `UNKNOWN_INVARIANT_VIOLATION_ERROR` (fallback is not allowed): "Trace replay finished but there are still some branches/dynamic calls not yet processed"`. With the fix, all transactions succeed.

## Key Areas to Review
<!--
- Identify any critical parts of the code that require special attention or understanding. Explain why these parts are crucial to the functionality or architecture of the project.
- Point out any areas where complex logic has been implemented. Provide a brief explanation of the logic and your approach to make it easier for reviewers to follow.
- Highlight any areas where you are particularly concerned or unsure about the code's impact on the change. This can include potential performance or security issues, or compatibility with existing features.
-->

## Type of Change
- [ ] New feature
- [x] Bug fix

items.title: [qs] Handle BatchV2 network messages
items.body:
## Description
<!-- Please include a summary of the change, including which issue it fixes or what feature it adds. Include relevant motivation, context and documentation as appropriate. List dependencies that are required for this change, if any. -->

This PR wires the handling of the new V2 Batch messages in the network and epoch manager -- `BatchMsgV2`, `SignedBatchMsgV2`, and `ProofOfStoreMsgV2`. The  `enable_batch_v2` flag is split into `enable_batch_v2_tx` and `enable_batch_v2_rx` to split the sending/receiving flags to make rollout safe and compatible.

Batch Requester returns V2 batch in a BatchResponse::BatchV2 enum when the batch if of type V2.

items.title: [Network] Add access control policies to network configs.
items.body:
## Description
This PR adds support for access control policies in the node network configs. With this, we can specify simple allow and block lists (based on peer IDs).

## Testing Plan
New and existing test infrastructure (as well as manual verification).


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Introduces configurable peer access control and wires it through the networking stack.
> 
> - Adds `AccessControlPolicy` (`allow_list`/`block_list`) with `is_peer_allowed()` and optional `NetworkConfig.access_control_policy` (default: none)
> - Plumbs policy via `NetworkBuilder` → `PeerManagerBuilder`/`ConnectivityManagerBuilder` into `PeerManager` and `ConnectivityManager`
> - Enforces policy:
>   - `ConnectivityManager::choose_peers_to_dial()` filters peers using `is_peer_allowed`
>   - `PeerManager` rejects inbound connections not permitted by the policy and increments rejection metrics
> - Extensive tests: YAML (de)serialization, policy evaluation, dialing filters, and peer manager acceptance/rejection
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e613069451638e46776342a9afea5622d8e7740b. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [compiler] New macros using abort with message
[Hot State] Enable pruner for hot state merkle db
items.body:
Just instantiate two more pruners, since we need to prune the hot state merkle
db as well. They share the same config with the existing state merkle db
pruners, since the window size should be the same.

Slight refactor to put all state db related pruners in a struct, since clippy is
(rightfully) complaining about having too many arguments in a few functions.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds pruning support for the hot state merkle DB and refactors state pruner wiring.
> 
> - Introduces `StatePruner` to group `state_merkle_pruner`, `epoch_snapshot_pruner`, `state_kv_pruner`, plus new optional `hot_state_merkle_pruner` and `hot_epoch_snapshot_pruner`
> - Wires pruner creation in `AptosDB::new_with_dbs`/`StateStore::new` and debugger path; updates accesses via `state_pruner`
> - On startup and snapshot commit, sets pruner target versions for all relevant pruners; persists min readable versions in writer
> - Updates reader/error checks to reference pruners through `state_pruner`; adjusts tests accordingly
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit eee11f0668cf0d67a18719b53c7e885cd8f2a4b5. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Expand PVSS benchmarking tables
items.body:

- Changed some bit size values from type `usize` to `u8`
- Removed duplicate `powers_of_two` code

items.title: [Orderbook] Address security review feedback
items.body:
Security improvements:
- Add cap on order_ids length in cleanup_expired_orders (max 100)
- Add bulk order depth limit of 30 per side to prevent gas DoS
- Disallow sequence_number 0 in bulk orders to avoid event ambiguity

Naming consistency:
- Rename cancel_details to cancel_reason in cancel_order
- Rename cancel_single_order_internal to cancel_taker_order_internal
- Rename size to new_size in decrease_order_size for clarity

Code cleanup:
- Remove unused BulkOrderRequestResponse enum
- Remove unused functions: is_single_order_active, get_mid_price, get_slippage_price
- Move get_bulk_order_remaining_size to bulk order section
- Change public functions to public(friend) where only used by friends

items.title: [tests][vm] Additional tests to increase code coverage of runtime reference safety checker
items.body:
## Description

In this PR, we add new masm tests for the runtime reference safety checker, focusing on negative tests (leading to invariant violations when static bytecode verifier is disabled and VM is run with reference safety checker enabled). 

With these tests, the effective line coverage of the runtime ref safety checker is close to 90%, significant improvement from what it was before.

## How Has This Been Tested?

New tests added, all of which were manually reviewed.

## Type of Change
- [x] Tests


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds a focused suite of transactional masm tests for the Move VM runtime reference safety checker, emphasizing negative cases (expected invariant violations) plus a few positive sanity checks to improve coverage.
> 
> - Introduces many new tests under `transactional-tests/tests/runtime_ref_checks` covering: local/global borrow conflicts, exclusive lock violations in calls and closures, reference poisoning via `write_ref`, `move_from`, `st_loc`, vector ops (`vec_swap`, `vec_pop_back`), and `copy_loc` behavior (immut OK, mut poisoned)
> - Validates nested/ancestor poisoning across deep structures, borrow sequencing, multiple immutable global borrows, proper `freeze_ref`, return rules (derived refs allowed; returning locals/globals rejected), and `move_to` consumption of signer refs
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 2e233af91dfaef61d51ebca19c1c3356516f0502. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [framework] replace with receiver-style call expr in non-spec code
items.body:
Split from the https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18423 for better PR granularity. 


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Modernizes Move codebase to receiver-style method calls, reducing `module::func(x, ...)` in favor of `x.func(...)`.
> 
> - Updates core modules like `aptos-framework/coin`, `code`, `governance`, `aptos_account`, `aptos_coin`, `block`, `base16`, `config_buffer`, `consensus_config`, etc.
> - Refactors experimental modules: `confidential_asset`, `veiled_coin`, `large_packages`, and trading (`market_bulk_order`, `dead_mans_switch_operations`), plus associated tests.
> - Adjusts docs to reflect new syntax (e.g., vector, option, table, smart_table, object method calls) and removes now-unneeded imports.
> 
> Emphasis: no functional/behavioral changes intended; call-site syntax only.

items.title: [vm] Support abort messages in native functions
items.body:
## Description
<!-- Please include a summary of the change, including which issue it fixes or what feature it adds. Include relevant motivation, context and documentation as appropriate. List dependencies that are required for this change, if any. -->

This PR adds support for abort messages in native functions.

Native function implementations can now call `SafeNativeError::abort_with_message` to abort with a message. These abort messages are propagated through the Move VM in the same way as abort messages originating from Move code.

### Scope

This change does **not** modify any existing native functions to include abort messages.

The large diff is primarily due to refactoring existing code from:

```
SafeNativeError::Abort { abort_code: … }
```
to
```
SafeNativeError::abort(…)
```

As a result, existing native functions behaviour remains completely unchanged. This PR only introduces the underlying support needed for future use of abort messages in native functions.

Note that abort messages are currently discarded by the VM (see `VMStatus::keep_or_discard` function) unless the `VM_BINARY_FORMAT_V10` feature is enabled.

## How Has This Been Tested?
<!--
- Please ensure that the functionality introduced by this change is well tested and verified to work as expected.
- Ensure tests cover both happy and unhappy paths.
- List and link relevant tests.
-->

- No new tests were added, since this feature is not used anywhere.
- All existing tests should pass.

## Key Areas to Review
<!--
- Identify any critical parts of the code that require special attention or understanding. Explain why these parts are crucial to the functionality or architecture of the project.
- Point out any areas where complex logic has been implemented. Provide a brief explanation of the logic and your approach to make it easier for reviewers to follow.
- Highlight any areas where you are particularly concerned or unsure about the code's impact on the chang

All checks should pass successfully with the updated action versions. ### Related Links ### Checklist - [ ] Have you ran `pnpm fmt`? - [ ] Have you updated the `CHANGELOG.md`? ---

items.title: [ans] Major version bump with ANS API improvements
items.body:
### Context

There were several issues with the current implementation of the ANS SDK:
- Pagination was not supported for large queries
- You couldn't use the ANS apis with the wallet adapter. There was a type conflict where trying to sign and submit transactions just wouldn't work. Now we return the raw function data for use in the wallet adapter (in lieu of updating the wallet standard to accept transactions). 
- User supplied query filters were overwritten by default filters 
- Name expiration did not take into consideration grace period, this is when a name is past expiration but only claimable by the current owner
- Name data was not fully standardized, in particular date formats and type exports. 

### Changes

This commit introduces breaking changes to the ANS API as part of version 6.0.0:

Breaking Changes:
- Replace GetANSNameResponse array return type with structured { names, total } objects for better type safety and pagination support
- Replace isActiveANSName() boolean function with getANSExpirationStatus() returning ExpirationStatus enum for more granular status tracking
- Update getName() to return AnsName | undefined instead of GetANSNameResponse[0] | undefined

New Features:
- Add ExpirationStatus enum (Active, InGracePeriod, Expired) for better expiration status handling
- Add grace period support allowing names to remain claimable by current owner during grace period
- Add AnsName interface extending RawANSName with enhanced fields including expiration_status and isInRenewablePeriod
- Export SubdomainExpirationPolicy enum from types module
- Add AnsTokenStandard type for tracking token standard version

The changes improve type safety, provide more granular expiration status information, and better support for grace period handling. This is a major
version bump (5.2.1 -> 6.0.0) as it breaks existing API contracts.

Updated CHANGELOG.md with all breaking changes and new features.


### Test Plan

I re-wrote the ANS

items.title: fix: Cross-chain UX improvements and bug fixes
items.body:
## Changes

- **Fix Solana USDC balance fetch**: Return "0" when wallet has no USDC token account instead of throwing an error
- **Add Solana Devnet warning**: Display warning in CCTP transfer UI reminding users to connect to Solana Devnet (not Testnet) when using testnet mode
- **Fix Sepolia RPC CORS**: Switch to PublicNode RPC endpoint that supports browser CORS requests

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> - **Fix Solana USDC balance fetch**: In `getUsdcBalance.ts`, return `"0"` when no USDC SPL token account exists and read balance from the first token account instead of falling back to the wallet address.
> - **Update Sepolia RPC (testnet)**: Switch default RPC to `https://ethereum-sepolia-rpc.publicnode.com` to resolve CORS issues.
> - **Minor cleanup**: Remove debug `console.log` of EVM `chainId` in `CCTPTransfer.tsx`.
> 
> *Changeset*: patch release for `@aptos-labs/cross-chain-core`.
>

items.title: Version Packages
items.body:
This PR was opened by the [Changesets release](https://github.com/changesets/action) GitHub action. When you're ready to do a release, you can merge this and the packages will be published to npm automatically. If you're not ready to do a release yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will be updated.


# Releases
## @aptos-labs/derived-wallet-ethereum@0.9.0

> - Adds `SolanaDerivedAccount` to `@aptos-labs/derived-wallet-solana@0.12.0` for programmatic signing with Solana keypairs

items.title: feat(derived-wallet-ethereum): add node support for programmatic signing
items.body:
## Summary

Add node support for programmatic signing with EIP1193DerivedAccount

- Added `EIP1193DerivedAccount` class for server-side/script-based signing
- Extracted reusable helper functions: `createMessageForEthereumTransaction` and `createAccountAuthenticatorForEthereumTransaction`
- Made `createSiweEnvelope` accept optional `domain` and `uri` parameters for Node.js compatibility
- Exported `defaultAuthenticationFunction` from shared module
- Added new `/node` entry point for Node.js-specific exports

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds Node/script-friendly programmatic signing using Ethereum keypairs and refactors signing utilities.
> 
> - Introduces `EIP1193DerivedAccount` for server-side signing via ethers `Wallet`; computes derived Aptos address and signs transactions using SIWE
> - Extracts helpers: `createMessageForEthereumTransaction` and `createAccountAuthenticatorForEthereumTransaction` for Node environments
> - Enhances `createSiweEnvelope` to accept optional `domain`/`uri`; updates `EIP1193DerivedPublicKey.verifySignature` to include `scheme` and domain/uri in SIWE envelope
> - Expands package exports (including `defaultEthereumAuthenticationFunction`, `EthereumAddress`) and updates README with Node usage; adds mainnet support table and minor doc cleanups
> - Refactors `signAptosTransaction` to use new helpers and explicit env-derived `domain`/`uri`/`scheme`
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit d445e925c17acd7cc4a24f0f8b29ebdde50a131d. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: feat(derived-wallet-solana): Add SolanaDerivedAccount for programmatic signing

If you're not ready to do a release yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will be updated.

items.title: Fix `TransactionContext` impls
items.body: Previously the impls were either insufficient, incomplete, or incorrect.

items.title: Add tests for transaction stream item timeout
Utilize gRPC response item timeout for all stream items instead of only version tracking
items.body: In production environments, the stream is forced to restart after 5 minutes due to API Gateway behavior. This is not the case when connecting to streams that do not go through API Gateway (i.e. localnet). In some cases, the stream will remain open but stuck. This change will utilize the timeout in config to restart the stream if transactions have not been received for that long. Previously the timeout was only used when waiting for the version tracking request.

items.title: Only track versions when new version detected
items.body: This makes it so logs aren't clogged by version tracking

items.title: Release version 1.2.0
items.body: Bump version to 1.2.0 and update the changelog to reflect new features, fixes, and improvements. ---

items.title: Aptos cli output artifacts
items.body: Add a GitHub Actions workflow and documentation to demonstrate building and using Aptos Move artifacts in CI/CD pipelines. Closes https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-cli/issues/20 ---

items.title: Pre-packaged cli version support
items.body: Add support for installing and updating a specific Aptos CLI version via the `APTOS_CLI_VERSION` environment variable. This allows users to pin to a particular CLI version, test against specific versions, or ensure reproducible builds in CI/CD pipelines by bypassing package managers and downloading directly from GitHub releases. Closes https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-cli/issues/8 ---

items.title: Exit code piping
items.body: Forward child process exit codes to the parent process. Previously, the CLI wrapper would always exit with code `0`, regardless of the actual exit code from the spawned CLI process, masking errors. This change ensures that non-zero exit codes from the child process are correctly propagated. Closes https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-cli/issues/21 ---

items.title: fix(holders): remove slow aggregate count query
items.body:
## Summary
Removes the slow `current_fungible_asset_balances_aggregate` count query that times out for tokens with many holders (e.g., USDT). Replaces numbered pagination with "Load more" button.

## Changes
- Remove aggregate count from `useGetCoinHolders` GraphQL query
- Replace `count` return value with `hasMore: boolean`
- Convert both HoldersTab components to use "Load more" pagination
- Use reducer pattern for accumulating holders across pages

## Test plan
- [x] Visit `/fungible_asset/0xbae207659db88bea0cbead6da0ed00aac12edcdda169e591cd41c94180b46f3b/holders?network=mainnet`
- [x] Verify page loads quickly without timeout
- [x] Click "Load more" and verify additional holders append

items.title: Website PWA support
items.body: ### Description This PR introduces full Progressive Web App (PWA) support to the website, enhancing user experience with installability, offline capabilities, and faster loading. Due to compatibility challenges with `vite-plugin-pwa` in the existing SSR environment (TanStack Start and Netlify), a custom service worker (`public/sw.js`) has been implemented. Key PWA features include: - **Intelligent Caching:** Utilizes a cache-first strategy for static assets and fonts, and a network-first strategy for API calls and HTML, with an offline fallback. - **Enhanced Manifest:** A comprehensive `manifest.json` with app details, icons, screenshots, and categories. - **Optimized Netlify Headers:** Configured `netlify.toml` for appropriate caching of PWA assets, ensuring `sw.js` updates immediately. ### Related Links ### Checklist ---
